summary: local-mcp ships install-time downloader and telemetry beacons to attacker-adjacent infrastructure
details: The package contains lifecycle/install-time JavaScript (download.js, index.js, setup.js) that combines `child_process`, `fs`, `os`, `http`, and `https` with hardcoded outbound destinations including `https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app` and `https://download.local-mcp.com`. download.js fetches binary content via `https.get` and writes it to disk; index.js performs `http.get`/`https.get` calls referencing `process.env` and `process.platform`; setup.js issues multiple `POST` requests across hundreds of lines mixed with `child_process` and `fs` access. The combination — opaque Railway-hosted endpoint, unverified install-time binary fetch from a non-registry CDN, environment/platform data forwarded to outbound calls, and shell-execution primitives in the same lifecycle path — matches the install-time-RCE / data-beaconing fingerprint and gives the publisher arbitrary code-execution authority over installer machines on every `npm install`. The package's domain (`local-mcp.com`) and the unrelated `office-mcp-production.up.railway.app` Railway app together form an unverified dropper/telemetry channel; no integrity check is performed on the downloaded payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
